steps.title: Identify the defining behavior
steps.content: The claim requires riders who actively prefer faster routes over alternatives. The strongest clue is a rider who chooses one route specifically because it reduces travel time.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice C demonstrates this directly: a daily work commuter who selects the faster bike lane over a scenic park path. The other three either don't commute to work or don't demonstrate speed-based route preferences. That matches choice C — C is correct.
